industries.name: Steel Alloying & High-Strength Steel
uses: Microalloying steel with 0.01–0.15% vanadium forms fine vanadium carbonitride (V(C,N)) precipitates that pin grain boundaries and precipitate harden the steel matrix — increases yield strength by 100–300 MPa with no decrease in weldability or toughness
High-strength low-alloy (HSLA) steel for structural beams, rebar, heavy plate, and pressure vessels uses vanadium microalloying to meet specifications impossible with plain carbon steel

Vanadium rebar (V-rebar) is now standard in seismic building codes in China — a national standard requiring higher yield strength rebar for earthquake resistance drove China to consume ~55% of world vanadium production
Tool steels (H13, D2, M2 high-speed steel): vanadium at 0.5–5% forms very hard vanadium carbide (VC) particles that resist abrasion and red hardness in cutting tools, dies, and molds
Ferrovanadium (FeV, 50–80% V) is the commercial form added to steelmaking — produced by aluminothermic reduction of V₂O₅ or by electric arc reduction of vanadium-bearing slag

companies.name: EVRAZ
companies.use: World's largest vanadium producer. Recovers vanadium from titaniferous magnetite iron ore at its Kachkanar mine and Nizhny Tagil steelworks in Russia. Produces vanadium slag processed to V₂O₅ and ferrovanadium.

companies.name: Largo Resources
companies.use: Operates the Maracás Menchen vanadium mine in Bahia, Brazil — one of the world's highest-grade primary vanadium deposits. Produces high-purity V₂O₅ flake for steel and vanadium redox battery markets.

companies.name: AMG Advanced Metallurgical Group
companies.use: Produces ferrovanadium and vanadium master alloys from petroleum refinery spent catalysts (a significant secondary vanadium source) and from primary vanadium oxide. Supplies steel mills and specialty alloy producers globally.

companies.name: Gulf Chemical (AMG subsidiary)
companies.use: Major processor of spent petroleum hydrodesulfurization (HDS) catalysts — vanadium accumulates in these catalysts during refinery use and is recovered by pyrometallurgical processing to produce vanadium pentoxide (V₂O₅).

industries.name: Vanadium Redox Flow Batteries (VRFB)
industries.share: ~8% of vanadium use — fastest growing segment
uses: Vanadium redox flow batteries store energy by cycling vanadium between V²⁺/V³⁺ (negative electrolyte) and V⁴⁺/V⁵⁺ (positive electrolyte) dissolved in sulfuric acid — unique among flow batteries in using only one element, eliminating cross-contamination
VRFB electrolyte acts as liquid energy storage — unlike lithium-ion, capacity (volume of electrolyte) and power (size of cell stack) are independently scalable, ideal for 4–12 hour grid storage
Unlimited cycle life (>20,000 cycles) with no capacity fade — vanadium ions are never deposited, just oxidized and reduced. Electrolyte retains full value at end of battery system life

Applications: grid-scale energy storage for renewable integration, peak shaving for industrial customers, and grid ancillary services (frequency regulation) where long duration and cycle durability outweigh lithium-ion
China has installed several large VRFB projects (200 MWh Dalian demonstration), and Australia, Japan, and Europe are developing utility-scale VRFB installations

companies.name: Invinity Energy Systems
companies.use: Leading commercial VRFB manufacturer. Produces modular vanadium flow battery systems for utility, commercial, and microgrid energy storage projects globally. Uses high-purity vanadium electrolyte supplied by primary producers.

companies.name: Rongke Power
companies.use: China's largest VRFB manufacturer. Built the 200 MW / 800 MWh Dalian Peak-Shaving Power Station — the world's largest flow battery installation as of 2022. Produces complete VRFB systems for Chinese and international grid projects.

companies.name: Largo Clean Energy (Largo Resources)
companies.use: Vanadium mine operator that has expanded into VRFB manufacturing. Produces VCHARGE± vanadium flow battery systems — vertically integrated from mining vanadium ore to delivering installed battery systems.

industries.name: Catalysts & Specialty Chemicals
uses: Vanadium pentoxide (V₂O₅) catalyst in the contact process for sulfuric acid production — converts SO₂ to SO₃ at 400–600°C over multiple catalyst beds; world sulfuric acid industry consumes thousands of tonnes of vanadium catalyst
Vanadium oxychloride (VOCl₃) as Ziegler-Natta polymerization catalyst for EPDM rubber (ethylene-propylene-diene monomer) and ethylene-propylene copolymer production
Vanadium-based selective catalytic reduction (SCR) catalysts (V₂O₅/TiO₂/WO₃) for NOₓ removal from power plant flue gas and diesel truck exhaust — converts NOₓ to N₂ and H₂O with ammonia reductant
Vanadium compounds used as drying catalysts in paints and inks, and as mordants in the textile dyeing industry

companies.name: Haldor Topsoe (Topsoe)
companies.share: ~35% of global sulfuric acid catalyst market
companies.use: World's leading supplier of vanadium-based sulfuric acid catalysts. Its VK series vanadium pentoxide catalysts are used in the majority of the world's sulfuric acid plants including the LEAP5™ high-activity low-temperature catalyst.

companies.name: BASF Catalysts
companies.share: ~25% of global sulfuric acid catalyst market
companies.use: Major vanadium catalyst producer for sulfuric acid, SCR NOₓ reduction, and specialty chemical applications. Supplies ring-pellet and Daisy-shaped V₂O₅ contact catalysts to fertilizer and chemical plant operators globally.
